#157 Circling Jeju nearly complete!

Areas discussed: Jeju South Korea, Jeju Olle Trail, Gunsan Oreum, Seogwipo, Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Terminal, Wolpyeong Port, Yakcheonsa Temple, Daepo Jusangjeollidae

October 15, 2025 1 hour 28 minutes Jeju Island, South Korea

All transcripts are machine generated.

So I pulled out my phone, did the whole Google translate, and it looks like as of 2022, there's a bridge coming up that they're working on. I don't know how long it takes to make a bridge, but I guess two years at least. Because it's 2024, is I record this? And apparently it's not done. And it said, yeah, you know, for your safety, please take this detour.

This must be weird, like for the person I just passed. I passed quite a few people farming. You know, I don't think this is a permanent Ole trail. They've just temporarily had to take on the burden of dickhead tours like me for this detour.

But yeah, it said, there's bridge construction coming up. And then when I looked ahead of me, there was also a just do not enter sign. Don't go this way sign for even the traffic. So that could explain why this coastal road has been so slight with the traffic.

And you know, as much as I like to struggle against the Ole trail and stick to the coast, I don't want to be a dick about it. You know, if it seems like it's just two options and one of the options is go inland and maybe see some stuff or stay by the coast and see the coast, I'm happy to do the coast. But if there's an actual reason, if there's a big sign, if there's a big warning, they're just saying, look, don't do this. Don't go that way. I'm not a fucking asshole. I'm not going to deny that. So here we go. I'm walking inland. I'm walking through a farm. Oh, and it is hot. I'm feeling it now. It's starting to build on me. Again, just then, just walking then. Don't think about it. And Quagmire takes out a great big wide brimmed floppy hat and puts it on. And the other two were like, what the hell is that? It's like, it's a sun hat. That's something else I noticed recently. It's like took a little while for it to sink in because it's hard sometimes to notice the absence of something. But I noticed in Seoul there were an absurd number of mirrors. Mirrors all over the place. Like there'd be all these, you know, the exercise equipment set up in the woods, which seemed like a bad joke in the wintertime. It feels a lot better than going into a gym. But it was the horrifying pollution of the wintertime. And you know, it's just like, this seems like a bad joke. where I came out from behind the caution tape. And the four young men took my lead and went down and passed the caution tape. It was right at that same spot. Yeah, basically the Ole trail had taken me in a figure eight, because from there...

I'd gone east up Goonson, but then it had taken me west back down Goonson, and then cut west to the centuries old horse trail, and then down to the coast. Oh, here's a barbecue restaurant with some lilting piano music.

And then popsicle one was the big 50% off, if you buy five, symbol came up, which again I translated that beforehand.
